gate.target_read: A source-boundary HIP/CK/rocWMMA candidate must beat the actual sparse-attention backward boundary, not just a late dKV-only slice.

tested_candidates.name: selected_tile_dq_scalar_atomic_dkv_group_atomic
tested_candidates.scope: source-boundary dQ+dKV; selected-tile CTA; scalar dQ partial atomics; group-aware dKV atomics
tested_candidates.read: This proves the output contract and build surface, but scalar dQ accumulation is a bring-up artifact and far too slow.

tested_candidates.name: selected_tile_dq_mfma_4wave_dkv_group_atomic
tested_candidates.scope: source-boundary dQ+dKV; selected-tile CTA; rocWMMA dQ tile; four D16 consumers; group-aware dKV atomics
tested_candidates.read: MFMA fixes the inner dQ math and cuts latency by about 1.70x versus scalar dQ, but selected-tile partial dQ accumulation still dominates.

tested_candidates.name: query_owned_dq_4wave
tested_candidates.scope: source-boundary dQ only; CTA owns token,H16,D64 and writes each dQ element once
tested_candidates.read: Owning dQ output removes global dQ atomics, but replays QK and dO.V score production across D groups and loses the score-page reuse that made the selected-tile producer viable.

tested_candidates.name: selected_tile_dq_mfma_8wave_dkv_group_atomic
tested_candidates.scope: source-boundary dQ+dKV; selected-tile CTA; rocWMMA dQ tile; eight D16 consumers; group-aware dKV atomics
tested_candidates.read: More same-workgroup D consumers help by about 1.26x versus four waves, but do not solve selected-tile dQ partial accumulation or dKV global atomics.

why: Selected-tile ownership preserves QK/dO.V score-page reuse, but dQ becomes many selected-tile partial writes or atomics.
Query-owned dQ writes once, but score-page recompute across D groups dominates.
Increasing D consumers inside one CTA helps, but not enough to approach the 11.13 ms sparse-attention boundary gate.

next_kernel_contract.required_shape: score page produced once for a token/selected window, consumed by enough D/H consumers before HBM, with dQ accumulation kept local or compact and dKV reduced by compressed group before global writes
avoid: materializing P or scaled_dP slabs to HBM
query-owned dQ that replays score production for every D group
selected-tile dQ scalar or MFMA partial atomics as a production path
late dKV-only replacements that arrive after PyTorch/Triton materialized selected state
next_kernel_contract.next_branch: Move below the current TileLang/CTA abstraction: prototype a persistent HIP or CK block-level design where a score-page producer feeds multiple output consumers inside one cooperative kernel, and use compact per-token or per-route partials only if the partial stream is bounded and reduced before HBM.

correctness.s4096_timing_runs: timing-only; correctness skipped because all tested variants are latency-rejected
correctness.reference_oracle: reference_dq/reference_dkv in probe_sparse_mla_source_boundary_hip_coop.py remains wired for follow-up correctness gates
